name: Transplant low-register half-panel staging into the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot

summary.name: Transplant low-register half-panel staging into the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot
summary.hypothesis: The richer NCU summary says the live queue should prioritize the only active family that directly attacks 200 registers per thread and the 16.63% active-warps ceiling. The correctness-safe 256x128 pivot should keep its current writer ownership, but transplant the compact staging and register-budget ideas that historically moved occupancy in the right direction.
summary.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with tensor_core_underutilization driven by register pressure and oversized live state
summary.risk: High: this is the best hotspot-aligned family, but it touches a broader geometry and could reintroduce correctness or shared-budget regressions if the old ownership split leaks back in.
ranking_notes: This family moved to rank 1 because the rich NCU handoff makes registers/thread the dominant hotspot instead of export bookkeeping.
It is ahead of the PTX-local retune because it is the only active family with explicit historical evidence of breaking the low-warp plateau.
The risk is real, but the queue needs at least one forward candidate that attacks the primary bottleneck directly.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if registers per thread stay at or above 200, if active warps do not improve, or if writer ownership changes trigger correctness failures.

target_hotspots.explanation: Launch Statistics is carrying metric Registers Per Thread.
Occupancy is carrying metric Achieved Occupancy.
GPU Speed Of Light Throughput is carrying metric Memory Throughput.

expected_local_changes: Compact the 256x128 live fragment footprint without reviving the broken half-panel writer split.
Reduce accumulator or staging lifetime before touching export-side arithmetic.
Keep the existing shared-memory budget discipline visible in the 256x128 path.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Tensor activity is part of the active bottleneck picture and should not drop after the next code edit.
Latency-hiding is already weak; active warps should not regress.
Long scoreboard stalls are consuming 7.20% of active warp issue slots.
Barrier stalls are consuming 5.47% of active warp issue slots.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
